data.body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/305d292b-a984-44c0-b89f-f8ed979d4c71)

1. team members only (to protect spam) and show `<UpgradeTeamsBadge />`
2.  don't check the team members calendar for conflict checking, only invite them
3. if possible mark them as "optional":

task.title: [CAL-5097] add the same "booking questions" to routing forms

data.title: [CAL-5097] add the same "booking questions" to routing forms
data.body:
right now "booking forms" for event-types have more (and different) inputs that routing forms:


event-types:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29c921ea-8677-4238-a198-55be00ce8188

routing forms:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cdaf70e0-7c5b-416b-a984-a57ded4c8525


we should reuse the entire event-type booking question UI inside routing forms.


we should also ask for the identifier **first** and not prefill it. (see event-types)

<sub>[CAL-5097](https://linear.app/calcom/issue/CAL-5097/add-the-same-booking-questions-to-routing-forms)</sub>

task.title: [CAL-4531] Take into account guest's availability when rescheduling

data.title: [CAL-4531] Take into account guest's availability when rescheduling
data.body:
If I (a Cal.com user) book another Cal.com user and they want to reschedule it on their end (instead of requesting me to reschedule), it doesn't take into account my availability and just lets them freely choose a time.

This is because the person who booked the meeting may or may not be a Cal.com user, hence we don't bother checking for their availability. But we do have the user's email, so we should look up the user and see if they're a Cal.com user, and then if so, retrieve the available times for them, and only display those when the host tries to reschedule

<sub>[CAL-4531](https://linear.app/calcom/issue/CAL-4531/take-into-account-guests-availability-when-rescheduling)</sub>

task.title: [CAL-3076] allow emails and invite people to a team event-type directly from "assignment" if not in team yet

data.title: [CAL-3076] allow emails and invite people to a team event-type directly from "assignment" if not in team yet
data.body:
- [ ] allow emails in assignment
- [ ] invite person if its not in the team yet (as Member)
- [ ] allow multiple emails comma separated: [user1@example.com](mailto:user1@example.com), [user2@example.com](mailto:user2@example.com)

task.title: Proton Calendar Integration

data.title: Proton Calendar Integration
data.body:
### Is your proposal related to a problem?

<!--
  Provide a clear and concise description of what the problem is.
  For example, "I'm always frustrated when..."
-->

for [Proton Calendar](https://proton.me/calendar) users, need to use .ics

### Describe the solution you'd like

<!--
  Provide a clear and concise description of what you want to happen.
-->

integration with [proton calendar](https://proton.me/calendar) for availabilities and meeting confirmations, finally I can give up on google altogether.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

<!--
  Let us know about other solutions you've tried or researched.
-->

manual process, or Google.

### Additional context

<!--
  Is there anything else you can add to the proposal?
  You might want to link to related issues here if you haven't already.
-->

the topic has been raised at https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/issues/3167
